summary: A sunny Santa Cruz show captured on a surprisingly clean audience tape, highlighted by Birdsong, Uncle John’s Band and an all‑time Brokedown Palace.
review: The performance is consistently praised – fans cite the bright Birdsong opening, a funky Deep Ellum stretch, dance‑able China>Rider, an intense Truckin' and a magical Brokedown Palace that caps a memorable second set.  The audience recording is described as “crisp” and almost soundboard quality, though one listener complained of cassette‑like degradation, making the overall listening experience very good.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating ≈4.3 with 14 reviews; most comments are enthusiastic about both show and tape quality, only a few isolated negatives.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean, crisp audience mix that many say approaches soundboard fidelity; occasional high‑treble roll‑off after Alabama noted, one reviewer described it as cassette‑like degradation.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set praised but second set described as the ‘wish‑list’ – a funky, danceable stretch highlighted by China>Rider and an intense Truckin', ending with a heartfelt Brokedown Palace.

band_member_comments.Jerry: One reviewer noted a friend was more impressed with Bob than Jerry; otherwise no specific comments about Jerry’s playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Several reviewers highlighted Bob Weir’s strong performance, calling it “very well” and noting his presence on the show.
